ogTitle: McMahons Point vs McMahons Reef: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in McMahons Point and McMahons Reef.
heading: McMahons Point vs McMahons Reef — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between McMahons Point, New South Wales and McMahons Reef, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: McMahons Point has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to McMahons Reef (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is McMahons Point or McMahons Reef safer from flooding?
faq.answer: McMahons Point has a flood risk score of 35/100, while McMahons Reef scores 59/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: McMahons Point scores 16/100 for bushfire risk. McMahons Reef scores 29/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in McMahons Point or McMahons Reef?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. McMahons Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
